revenue: $28.09 billion (up sequentially from $26.46 billion; 9-month revenue up 7.0% YoY)
net_income: $8.47 billion (up sequentially from $7.12 billion; 9-month net income up 12.3% YoY)
key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $1.06 (up from $0.81 in Q3 2024)
Return on average tangible common shareholders' equity (ROTCE) of 15.43%
Provision for credit losses of $1.30 billion
qoq_changes: Total revenue increased to $28.09 billion from $26.46 billion in Q2 2025
Net income grew to $8.47 billion from $7.12 billion in Q2 2025
Provision for credit losses decreased to $1.30 billion from $1.59 billion in Q2 2025
new_risks: Ongoing consumer and commercial credit risk exposure, maintaining a $1.30 billion provision for credit losses
Continued monitoring of climate risk and interest rate risk in the banking book
management_tone: Bullish, driven by strong sequential growth in revenue and net income, alongside a reduction in quarter-over-quarter credit loss provisions.
analyst_note: Bank of America delivered a strong Q3 2025, marked by $28.1 billion in revenue and a solid 15.43% ROTCE. The sequential decline in credit loss provisions suggests stabilizing credit quality, supporting robust bottom-line growth.
